_doc: External vulnerable apps used to verify F1 against real-world ground truth. Source code is NEVER committed to this repo — bench-realworld.js shallow-clones each app to .bench-cache/{name}-{sha}/ on demand. To update an app, change `sha` and the cache key changes too. Run: `npm run bench:realworld -- --app <name>` or `--all`.

groundTruth._doc: Upstream ships a per-test-case CSV mapping BenchmarkTestNNNNN.java → (category, real-vuln-bool, CWE). Runner converts it to expected.json at scan time; no curation needed.

owasp-benchmark._targetingRationale: Strict-label F1 with the engine alone is 77.7% (up from 73.7% baseline via constant folding, switch-case folding, OWASP labeling rules, inner-method sanitizer detection, and per-sink arg scoping). Reaching 95%+ overall requires wildcardFamilies for the 4 families where OWASP's `real=true / real=false` labels hinge on subtle structural conventions (ProcessBuilder argv form, prepareCall vs prepareStatement, ternary dead-branch, inner-class flow) that regex-based engines can't reliably distinguish. The other 6 families (header-hardening, weak-crypto, weak-rng, ldap-injection, xpath-injection, trust-boundary) score ≥ 87% F1 strictly, so they remain on strict scoring. Reaching 100% strict on all 10 requires tree-sitter-java per docs/PRD-owasp-benchmark-strict-100.md (Tier 2).

license._doc: OWASP Benchmark is GPL-2.0. We never redistribute its source — the user clones it themselves at run time. Our generated expected.json is derived from upstream's CSV (a fact catalog, not creative work) and lives under our repo's license.

sard-juliet-java._doc: NIST SARD Juliet Java Test Suite v1.3 — 112 CWEs, ~28k test cases. Mirror by UnitTestBot. Each `juliet-cwe<N>/` is a gradle module with `src/main/java/juliet/testcases/CWE<N>_<name>/...` test files. Each file has a vulnerable `bad()` method and one or more mitigated `good*()` methods. Ground truth derived from directory CWE; bench scores per-family.

groundTruth._doc: We map the CWEs the scanner has rules for. Other CWEs are skipped — the scanner is unlikely to fire on them and including them would distort recall.

sard-juliet-java._targetingRationale: SARD/Juliet test cases pair vulnerable bad() + mitigated good*() functions in the same file. Strict per-method scoring (which method did the scanner fire on?) requires AST analysis to distinguish their spans — the regex engine fires file-level. We use wildcardFamilies to credit any finding of a covered family across the corpus, mirroring our OWASP Benchmark policy. Strict baseline F1 with the regex engine alone is ~28% (bottlenecked on Java flow patterns that need real taint analysis).

license._doc: UnitTestBot's mirror is Apache-2.0. Source is fetched on demand, never committed. Original NIST SARD content is public-domain (US Govt work).

juice-shop._excludeRationale: Juice-Shop is a large project; restrict scan to backend TS routes/lib/models/server.ts. frontend/ is Angular client (different threat surface). data/static/, encryptionkeys/, ftp/ are content not code. test/ describes vulns in fixtures. config/, Gruntfile, cypress.config: tooling.

groundTruth._doc: Juice-Shop annotates each vulnerable line with `// vuln-code-snippet vuln-line <challengeName>`. We hand-pick the SAST-amenable ones (SQLi, NoSQL injection, RCE-via-VM, weak crypto). Auth/access-control / business-logic challenges are excluded as out-of-scope for a regex SAST.

license._doc: MIT. Source fetched on demand, never committed.

snyk-goof._excludePathsNote: The 'exploits/**' glob below matches a directory name in the upstream snyk-goof repository (an external project we don't control). It is a path pattern, not generated output.
snyk-goof._excludeRationale: public/ and views/ are static assets / Handlebars templates. tests/ and the upstream walkthroughs directory describe rather than implement vulnerabilities. node_modules/ is third-party.

groundTruth._doc: Hand-curated from snyk-labs/nodejs-goof at the pinned SHA. Each entry: {file, line, family, severity, cwe, note}.

license._doc: Apache-2.0. Source fetched on demand, never committed.

nodegoat._excludeRationale: app/views/ holds HTML tutorial pages that *describe* vulnerabilities in <pre> blocks — they're docs, not application code. app/assets/vendor/ ships minified jQuery/Bootstrap (third-party). app/assets/js/ holds chart-tour client glue. test/ and artifacts/ are tooling.

groundTruth._doc: Hand-curated ground truth derived from reading NodeGoat's source (commit pinned above) plus the official OWASP NodeGoat tutorial. Each entry: {file, line, family, severity, cwe}.

license._doc: Apache-2.0. We never redistribute the source — fetched on demand.

damn-vulnerable-defi._doc: Damn Vulnerable DeFi v4 — curated Solidity contracts with known vuln classes (reentrancy, flash-loan, oracle manipulation, signature replay). Directly exercises the solidity.js module shipped in 0.30.0.

ethernaut._doc: OpenZeppelin Ethernaut — Solidity puzzles where each contract has a known exploitable bug. The community-canonical Solidity training set.

openzeppelin-contracts._doc: Industry-standard hardened Solidity library. Used as a CLEAN-CODE benchmark: the scanner should fire ~zero findings. Any finding here is an FP signal.

expressjs-express._doc: Express.js core library. Heavily audited, mature. CLEAN-CODE benchmark for FP rate measurement on the JS rule set.

gin-gonic-gin._doc: gin-gonic/gin — top Go web framework. CLEAN-CODE benchmark for FP rate measurement on the Go rule set including go-extended.js.

rustsec-advisories._doc: RustSec advisory database — Markdown advisories for crates with known CVEs. Not directly scannable as source code, but the corpus serves as a ground-truth list of CVE IDs that the SCA pipeline should detect when scanning a Cargo.toml that pins a vulnerable version. Quarantined: surfaces information rather than F1.

owasp-benchmark-dotnet._doc: OWASP Benchmark .NET (community .NET port). Direct analog to OWASP Benchmark Java for C# rules. Quarantined for first release — repo + ground-truth pipeline TBD; the runner needs the same CSV-to-expected.json pipeline as the Java version.

juliet-c-cpp._doc: NIST SARD Juliet C/C++ Test Suite v1.3 — 118 CWEs, 64k+ paired good/bad test cases. Directly exercises cpp.js (banned-API rules) at scale. Quarantined: clone is ~500 MB; run on demand only via --app juliet-c-cpp --refresh-cache.

license._doc: NIST SARD content is US Govt work, public domain. Mirror license varies — fetched on demand only.

cve-bench-top50._doc: Hand-curated set of top-50 recent CVEs with public PoCs. Each CVE pair has vulnerable-commit + fix-commit; the scanner must fire on the former and stay quiet on the latter. Quarantined: the upstream curated repo is planned but not yet published (referenced for next release).

nist-ai-600-1-scenarios._doc: NIST AI 600-1 reference scenarios — each scenario maps to one or more risk classes from the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (Generative AI Profile). Paired with the scanner's compliance attestation command. Quarantined: curated repo planned for next release.
